classification.spec_type_rationale: Zero-behavior-change file move + import-path update + constitution/doc classification update; all tests green, no contract/prop change. Textbook refactor; overrides research's feature_addition pre-seed.

spec_seeds.overview: Reorganize the renderer's flat components/organisms/ folder so it stays navigable as later epics (C response, E persistence/collections/history, F navigation) add organisms toward a ~25-file count. Adopt a domain-placement convention in constitution §2.2, then apply it minimally: move the one genuinely shared primitive (Divider) to molecules/, group the shell-domain organisms under organisms/shell/, and keep Sidebar + TabBar as flat domain singletons. The change is pure file relocation + import-path updates with zero behavior change — all tests stay green.

acceptance_criteria.statement: The reorganized renderer component tree shall contain no barrel or index re-export file in components/molecules/, components/organisms/, or components/organisms/shell/.
The renderer source tree shall place the Divider component and its sibling stylesheet under components/molecules/ rather than components/organisms/.
The renderer source tree shall place Shell, Titlebar, Statusbar, and PaneSplit (component and stylesheet files) under components/organisms/shell/ rather than directly under components/organisms/.
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: for n in Shell Titlebar Statusbar PaneSplit; do test -f src/renderer/src/components/organisms/shell/$n.tsx && test -f src/renderer/src/components/organisms/shell/$n.css && ! test -e src/renderer/src/components/organisms/$n.tsx || exit 1; done

acceptance_criteria.statement: The renderer source tree shall retain Sidebar and TabBar directly under components/organisms/ as flat domain singletons.
The moved components shall preserve their public export surface, prop types, and runtime behavior unchanged by the relocation.
WHILE the user drags a pane or sidebar divider, the system shall write the same CSS custom properties to the document root element as before the move.
The full Vitest unit suite and Playwright component-test suite shall pass without any modification to test assertions.
The constitution renderer-tier section shall record the domain-placement rule as a single labelled rule that extends the existing tier-layering canon.
The constitution membership tree and the UI-primitives entity list shall both reflect the new component layout in lockstep.
The architecture documentation UI-primitives table and dependency diagram shall reflect the new component layout.
The renderer shall contain no import that references a moved component at its former organisms path.
The renderer type-check shall pass for both the node and web TypeScript configurations.
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: npm run typecheck:node && npm run typecheck:web

acceptance_criteria.statement: The linter shall pass on all changed files.

ac_subsection_na.behavior_change: Zero-behavior-change refactor: no runtime behavior changes; only file locations, import paths, and documentation change.
ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI or pipeline configuration is added or changed by this reorganization.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No git hooks or quality gates are added or changed; existing type-check/lint/build gates are reused unchanged.

constraints.content: renderer component tiers flow downward only: organisms -> molecules -> atoms; no sibling-tier or upward imports
every change impacts as little code as possible; never fix unrelated code you happen to see
renderer imports cross-module code via the @renderer alias rather than deep relative paths
Move each relocated component's sibling .css and co-located __tests__/ fixtures together with its .tsx, and update all @renderer import paths in both production callers and test/story fixtures.
Make no logic, prop, export-surface, or visual change; keep the work to one logical refactor commit per Conventional Commits.
Preserve the Shell store-to-documentElement CSS-var contract and the Divider ratio-valued drag mapping exactly (PaneSplit composes Divider for the pane ratio).

affected_areas.impact: Move both files to src/renderer/src/components/molecules/; sibling .css travels with the .tsx. Resolves the §2.2:56 sibling-tier import violation.
Move all four components + sibling .css into new src/renderer/src/components/organisms/shell/; PaneSplit co-locates with its sole consumer Shell.
Stay flat under organisms/; only their @renderer import of the moved Divider (Sidebar) is rewritten. No file move.

Rewrite @renderer paths to moved files: App.tsx:3 (Shell); Sidebar.tsx:39 (Divider); Shell.tsx:78/80/81 (Titlebar/PaneSplit/Statusbar); PaneSplit.tsx:40 (Divider). Shell's import of flat Sidebar is unchanged.

Rewrite @renderer paths to moved files: Shell.test.tsx:23/25/26/27/28 + Shell.stories.tsx:14/15/17/18/19 (Shell/Titlebar/Statusbar/PaneSplit/Divider); Tabs.stories.tsx:45 (cross-file Shell.css import). Move co-located test/story fixtures with their component as needed.

Add the labelled domain-placement rule inside §2.2; update the §2.2 EXAMPLE membership tree (l.70 region) and the §5.1 UI-primitives list (l.198) to the new layout. Conscious update — no silent config drift.
Update the renderer UI-Primitives Layer table + mermaid Dependency Overview so the third classification site matches the new layout (per the in-feature docs decision).

out_of_scope.content: Promoting PaneSplit to molecules/ — that would require a non-zero-change prop rename (request/response → primary/secondary) to strip its domain vocabulary; deferred to a separate future change.
Choosing PaneSplit's long-term home (organisms/shell/ now vs a future organisms/workspace/ or organisms/response/) — revisited when epic C ships.
Creating domain subfolders for future epics (response, persistence, navigation) ahead of need — a domain folder is created only when it reaches >=2 components (no empty future folders).
Moving Sidebar or TabBar into domain subfolders — both stay flat domain singletons.
Any back-compat re-export shim or barrel/index file at the old organisms/ paths.
Any runtime, logic, styling, or accessibility behavior change to the moved components.

risks.risk: Import-graph breakage if any path missed (caught by tsc zero-unresolved + build); CSS co-location/load-order must travel with each .tsx (Shell.css/TabBar.css coupling proven brittle in 005); test/story import paths must move too. All mechanically detectable — no silent runtime risk.
A moved-file import path is missed, breaking the renderer import graph.
risks.mitigation: Type-check (both configs, zero unresolved) plus the production build statically catch every dangling import before completion; the §5.7 grep AC asserts no old-path import survives.

risks.risk: A moved component's sibling .css or a cross-file CSS coupling (e.g. Tabs.stories.tsx importing Shell.css) is dropped, silently degrading visuals.
risks.mitigation: Move each .css atomically with its .tsx, rewrite the cross-file css import, and rely on the Playwright CT fidelity suite to catch visual regression.

risks.risk: The three classification sites (constitution membership tree, constitution UI-primitives list, architecture doc) drift out of lockstep.
risks.mitigation: Update all three sites in the same change; §5.6 ACs assert each reflects the new layout.
